metadata.title:
DRY FARMING MEN
TO Be GUESTS OF


WAVERLy FARMERS

pages.raw_text:
, John T. Burns, secretary-treas-
urer of the dry farming congress,
and R. w. Thatcher, director of
agriculture of Washington Agricul-
tural college and member OF the
board of governors of the dry farm
ing congress, will be guests at the
Farmers Union, No. 55, at Waverly.
next Thursday at special meet
ing, banquet and farmers lnstl-
tute, arrangements for which are
being made by A. D. Thayer, mayor
of Waverly and member of the
Washington board of control OF the
congress.


The Farmers Union will meet at
10 o'clock in the morning for
two hours session. At 2 o'clock
in the afternoon dinner will be
served, at which Mr.. Burns and
Professor Thatcher will be the
guests of honor The dinner Will
be followed with an institute ses-
sion.


Mr.. Burns will speak on the dry
farming congress and It's work, lay
ing special stress on the Importance
and significance of the fifth annual
session, which will be held here the
second week in October. Professor
Thatcher will discuss conservation
problems and dry farming methods.


This is the first of series OF
meetings to be held in various parts
of the state for the purpose OF
bringing the dry farming congress
and it's work directly to the atten-
ton of the farmers of this state.
Mayor Thayer says the farmers In
that vicinity were deeply Interested
in the work and were planning for
liberal representation in the Inter
national Dry Farming exposition, to
take place here IN connection with
the fifth congress.
